length.description: Returns the number of characters in string (not bytes).

match.description: Tests if string matches the glob pattern (*, ?, [chars]).

range.description: Returns the substring from first to last index (inclusive).

first.description: Returns the index of the first occurrence of needleString in haystackString.

last.description: Returns the index of the last occurrence of needleString in haystackString.

index.description: Returns the character at charIndex (supports end-±N).

trim.description: Removes leading and trailing characters (defaults to whitespace).

totitle.description: Capitalizes the first character of string.

equal.description: Returns 1 if strings are equal, 0 otherwise.

is.description: Tests if string belongs to a character class (alnum, alpha, ascii, boolean, digit, double, integer, list, lower, upper, space, wideinteger, wordchar, xdigit).

map.description: Replaces substrings according to key-value pairs in mapping.

replace.description: Replaces characters from first to last with newString.

reverse.description: Returns string with characters in reverse order.

cat.description: Concatenates all arguments into a single string (Tcl 8.6+).

wordstart.description: Returns the index of the start of the word containing charIndex.

wordend.description: Returns the index of the character just after the word containing charIndex.

bytelength.description: Returns the number of bytes in string (deprecated, use encoding + string length).

join.description: Joins path components with the platform separator.

dirname.description: Returns the directory portion of a path.

tail.description: Returns the filename portion of a path (last component).

extension.description: Returns the file extension including the dot.

exists.description: Returns 1 if file or directory exists, 0 otherwise.

isfile.description: Returns 1 if name is a regular file.

isdirectory.description: Returns 1 if name is a directory.

mkdir.description: Creates directories and all necessary parent directories.

rename.description: Renames or moves a file or directory.

nativename.description: Returns the platform-specific native form of the path.

normalize.description: Returns an absolute path with . and .. resolved.

stat.description: Stores file status information (atime, ctime, dev, gid, ino, mode, mtime, nlink, size, type, uid) in array varName.

size.description: Returns the file size in bytes.

readable.description: Returns 1 if the file is readable by the current user.

writable.description: Returns 1 if the file is writable by the current user.

executable.description: Returns 1 if the file is executable.

mtime.description: Returns or sets the last modification time (Unix epoch seconds).

atime.description: Returns or sets the last access time (Unix epoch seconds).

type.description: Returns the type of file: file, directory, characterSpecial, blockSpecial, fifo, link, or socket.

exists.description: Returns 1 if the named variable exists in the current scope.

commands.description: Returns names of visible commands matching pattern.

procs.description: Returns names of visible procedures matching pattern.
procs.example: info procs calc_*  ;# → list of proc names

args.description: Returns the argument list of the named procedure.

body.description: Returns the body of the named procedure.

level.description: Returns the current stack level or info about a specific level.

script.description: Returns the name of the currently executing script file.

hostname.description: Returns the name of the machine.

patchlevel.description: Returns the exact version of the Tcl interpreter (e.g., 8.6.12).

globals.description: Returns names of global variables matching pattern.

locals.description: Returns names of local variables matching pattern.
locals.example: info locals  ;# → list of local var names

vars.description: Returns names of all visible variables (local + global via upvar/global).
vars.example: info vars  ;# → list of visible var names

default.description: Returns 1 if arg has a default value, stores it in varName.

exists.description: Returns 1 if arrayName is an array variable.

get.description: Returns a list of key-value pairs matching pattern.
get.example: array get opts  ;# → key1 val1 key2 val2

set.description: Sets array elements from a key-value list.

names.description: Returns element names matching pattern. Mode: -exact, -glob, -regexp.

size.description: Returns the number of elements in the array.

unset.description: Unsets array elements matching pattern (all if no pattern).

statistics.description: Returns statistics about the array hash table distribution.

create.description: Creates a new dictionary from key-value arguments.

get.description: Returns the value for the given key path.

set.description: Sets a value in a dictionary variable at the given key path.

exists.description: Returns 1 if the key path exists in the dictionary.

unset.description: Removes the entry at the given key path from the dictionary.

remove.description: Returns a new dictionary with the specified keys removed.

replace.description: Returns a new dictionary with specified key-value pairs replaced.

merge.description: Merges multiple dictionaries (later values override earlier).

keys.description: Returns a list of keys matching pattern.

values.description: Returns a list of values for keys matching pattern.

size.description: Returns the number of key-value pairs.

map.description: Maps over dictionary, collecting body results into a new dict (Tcl 8.7+).

filter.description: Filters dictionary by key, value, or script.

info.description: Returns internal information about the dictionary.

update.signature: dict update dictVar key varName ?key varName ...? body
update.description: Updates dictionary entries from variables after executing body.
update.example: dict update opts voltage v { set v [expr {$v * 2}] }

with.description: Creates variables from dictionary keys, executes body, writes back.
